title: azorella echinus
intro: Azorella echinus, also known as Raoul's azorella or Raoul's lungwort, is a flowering plant native to the mountains of New Zealand. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, allowing it to be effective in treating respiratory issues and wounds. The health benefits of Azorella echinus include soothing coughs and sore throats, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Traditionally, herbal preparations of Azorella echinus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
